open_questions: Substitute verified holder entry price for reference-price yield curve
Download CVR Agreement and future Milestone Notices to local `investor-documents/`
Promote any third-party CVR specialist source before numeric blend
thesis: ABMD.CVR is not an operating company. It is the **contingent value right** issued to former Abiomed shareholders when Johnson & Johnson completed its acquisition on **December 22, 2022**. **[Fact]** Each CVR is a non-tradeable contractual claim to receive up to **$35.00** in cash if three defined commercial and clinical milestones are achieved under the CVR Agreement. **[Fact]** Holders received one CVR for each Abiomed share tendered at **$380.00** upfront cash. **[Fact]** Approximately **25.76 million** shares were tendered (~57% of then-outstanding Abiomed stock). **[Fact]** Abiomed now operates as a standalone business within JNJ MedTech; the CVR is a tail cash option on Impella net sales and regulatory/clinical success, not equity in JNJ.
why_market_wrong: The CVR is **non-tradeable** and largely invisible to screens: broker quotes show **0.000** with no volume, so holders who still own the claim may undervalue live milestone optionality or over-discount JNJ measurement discretion. **[Inference]** Predictive attribute: **complexity discount** on a dated, multi-trigger cash stack. **[Inference]** Pre-acquisition Abiomed revenue was **$1.032 billion** (FY2022); the net-sales milestone requires **$3.7 billion** in a defined JNJ fiscal window, so the market may treat the **$17.50** leg as unreachable while underweighting partial **$8.75** delayed payment or clinical legs. **[Inference]** Conversely, holders may anchor on the **$35** headline and ignore simultaneous probability and timing discounting. Proof-first component math sums to about **$13.44/CVR** base economic value at explicit milestone probabilities.
